summary: Validate M4 deterministic campaign replay, combat replay, and runtime persistence boundaries
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic replay fixture covers the complete M4 loop from objective creation through muster, supply, route, march, automatic engagement/siege, withdrawal or success, and postwar candidate creation.
The same seed and command sequence produce the same M4 state hash across Node runner and browser Worker paths used by the project.
At least one automatic engagement/siege outcome is replayable from command/event tail or explicit fixture inputs, with stable casualty/supply/result hashes.

M4 runtime state is either explicitly persisted/loaded through save-format-compatible DTOs or explicitly rejected with safe compatibility errors until the accepted persistence path exists; no silent data loss is permitted.
The task does not add product gameplay, UI design, telemetry, Electron/Node integration, networking, or arbitrary-code mod behavior.
required_tests: Node/browser Worker replay command proves identical M4 state hashes for the same campaign command sequence.
Replay fixture covers automatic engagement/siege and postwar candidate output.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-DETERMINISM-REPLAY-001

test_engineer.responsibility: primary M4 deterministic replay and persistence-boundary validation writer

qa_reviewer.responsibility: independent read-only QA review for M4 deterministic replay validation
qa_reviewer.status_note: Writer REVIEW handoff routed by lead; QA review in progress.

notes: This task validates determinism and persistence boundaries; it should not broaden M4 gameplay.
If save-format changes are required, keep them explicit, schema-backed, and reviewed; stop if compatibility risk exceeds task scope.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
